---
title: ""
about:
  template: trestles
  image: assets/profile.jpg
  image-width: 14em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:denis.d.patterson@durham.ac.uk
    - icon: file-earmark-text
      text: CV
      href: assets/cv.pdf
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=MySHM30AAAAJ&hl=en
    - text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4509-7294
    - icon: building
      text: Durham Profile
      href: https://www.durham.ac.uk/staff/denis-d-patterson/
---

## Denis Patterson

I am an Assistant Professor in the [Department of Mathematical Sciences](https://www.durham.ac.uk/departments/academic/mathematical-sciences/) at [Durham University](https://www.durham.ac.uk/).

My research spans **nonlinear dynamics**, **stochastic analysis**, and **mathematical modelling**, with applications to ecology, developmental biology, and epidemiology. I am particularly interested in spatial models of vegetation dynamics, alternative stable states and tipping points in ecological systems, and within-host models of malaria immunity.

Before joining Durham, I was a postdoctoral researcher at Brandeis University with [Prof. Jonathan Touboul](https://scholarworks.brandeis.edu/esploro/profile/jonathan_touboul/overview) and subsequently at Princeton University in the group of [Prof. Simon Levin](https://slevin.princeton.edu/). I completed my PhD in Applied Mathematics at Dublin City University under the supervision of [Prof. John Appleby](https://www.dcu.ie/maths/people/john-appleby).

I am always happy to hear from prospective PhD students and postdoctoral researchers interested in working with me — please [get in touch](mailto:denis.d.patterson@durham.ac.uk) or see the [Projects & Students](people.qmd) page for more information.
